Registry / Terms / detection limit

detection limit

—Not in VIM/VIML G 18 #00151 · 13 instances

Suggested actions

  1. HIGH No authoritative definition in VIM or VIML.
  2. MEDIUM 9 distinct definitions — harmonise.

Publication instances

Ed.YearPublicationClauseDefinitionConsistency
20102006OIML R 82:2006
#01055
3.11
mass flow rate (for mass flow rate dependent detectors) or concentration (for concentration-dependent detectors) yielding a signal equal to three times the short-term noise level as determined on a statistical basis
pending
20102006OIML R 83:2006
#01069
3.9
minimum amount of a specific compound which, when injected Some compounds do not give a molecular ion, into the gas chromatograph, produces a signal-to-noise ratio of at bu least 3 for a characteristic base peak of that compound in depending on the mode of ionization and over a specified mass intense range
pending
20102006OIML R 116:2006
#01524
3.11
concentration of an element that provides an output signal equivalent to three times the standard deviation of the mean of the output signal of a blank solution and is the minimum quantity measured before it can be concluded that an element has been detected
pending
20101997OIML R 123:1997
#01606
3.2
concentration of an element which provides an output signal equivalent to three times the standard deviation of the background. It is the minimum quantity of an element that can be detected. For this Recommendation, it may be derived from the statistical variation in the background using the following equation: D = 3 (B/t)1/2/S where: D = detection limit (unit of concentration); B = background (counts per second); t = measurement time (seconds); S = sensitivity (counts per second per unit of concentration).
pending
20101994OIML R 113:1994
#01496
3.9
the smallest quantity of a sample component which yields a detector output signal that is three times greater than the short- term noise. Note:
pending
20101991OIML D 22:1991
#00151
3.6
the concentration of a substance that will yield a mean output signal equal to three times the mean short term noise level, as determined on a statistical basis
pending
20101991OIML R 100:1991
#01198
3.6
the concentration of an element that provides a signal equivalent In this Recommendation, this term refers to to three times the standard deviation of the baseline noise as determined on a statistical basis. It is the minimum quantity measured before it can be concluded that an element has been detected
pending
202X2006OIML R 82:2006
#01055
3.11
mass flow rate (for mass flow rate dependent detectors) or concentration (for concentration-dependent detectors) yielding a signal equal to three times the short-term noise level as determined on a statistical basis
pending
202X2006OIML R 83:2006
#01069
3.9
minimum amount of a specific compound which, when injected into the gas chromatograph, produces a signal-to-noise ratio of at least 3 for a characteristic base peak of that compound depending on the mode of ionization and over a specified mass range
pending
202X2006OIML R 116:2006
#01524
3.11
concentration of an element that provides an output signal equivalent to three times the standard deviation of the mean of the output signal of a blank solution and is the minimum quantity measured before it can be concluded that an element has been detected
pending
202X1997OIML R 123:1997
#01606
3.2
concentration of an element which provides an output signal equivalent to three times the standard deviation of the background. It is the minimum quantity of an element that can be detected. For this Recommendation, it may be derived from the statistical variation in the background using the following equation: D = 3 (B/t)1/2/S where: D = detection limit (unit of concentration); B = background (counts per second); t = measurement time (seconds); S = sensitivity (counts per second per unit of concentration).
pending
202X1994OIML R 113:1994
#01496
3.9
the smallest quantity of a sample component which yields a detector output signal that is three times greater than the short- term noise.
pending
202X1991OIML D 22:1991
#00151
3.6
the concentration of a substance that will yield a mean output signal equal to three times the mean short term noise level, as determined on a statistical basis
pending
